MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Julia Butler, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for Michael Schmierer, Temporary Attorney-in-Fact.
Jerome Garchik, Esq., in person, for Tara Houston, Objector.
Darice Murray-McKay, in person, in propria persona Objector.

The court at the outset acknowledges the ex parte application FOR ORDER TO DISCHARGE TEMPORARY ATTORNEY IN FACT MICHAEL SCHMIERER, etc., that is pending in the court's e-filing queue. Counsel and Ms. Murray-Mckay presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the subject matters. There is discussion regarding possibly scheduling an additional mediation and the parties contacting the mediator accordingly.

After additional discussion, all subject matters are continued to May-06-2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Verified Petition For 1)Misuse Of Power Of Attorney Under Probate Code Section 4231.5, 4540 And 4541 2)Revocation Of Authority Of Respondent As Attorney In Fact Under Probate Code Section 4541(D) 3) Accounting And Surcharge By Respondent As Attorney In Fact For A Principal Under Probate Code Section 4541(C) 4) Elder Financial Abuse 5) Negligence/Negligence Per Se
Off Calendar Jan-08-2024 Continued To Mar-11-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Jan-08-2024 Continued To Mar-11-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jerome Garchik for Tara Houston (respondent).
Julia Butler appeared by zoom for Michael Schmierer (appointed temporary attorney-in-fact).
Darice Murray McKay appeared by zoom in propria persona.
Ganzorig Batsuuri (son in law of Tom Houston) also present in propria persona.

Mr. Garchik stated that they had mediation, that they made certain progress, but they were unable to conclude all of the issues, and requested 60-days to see if they can get counsel to substitute in for the pro per parties. Mrs. Butler stated that Mr. Schmierer would like to be discharged, that he has marshalled most of the accounts and has provided stability to the family, and that it is his opinion that it would not be appropriate for him to continue because the family is very private and does not welcome an outsider. Mrs. Butler also stated that they think it would be better for a family member to act for Mr. Houston, that she was informed over the weekend that Mr. Batsuuri is willing to step up, that he is already acting as attorney-in-fact for Camille, that she needs to get her client's bond discharged and that they can file a stipulation to have Mr. Batsuuri step in. Mr. Garchik stated that there is a very large claim against Mrs. Murray, that it is his understanding that she is already considering bankruptcy, that Mr. Houston is now residing at the home with the family, that Mr. Houston lacks capacity to manage his finances, but has no physical disabilities and that Camille is 82 years of age and has given power of attorney to her son-in-law (Mr. Batsuuri). The court noted that two consents to electronic service were signed by Mr. Batsuuri, but it does not have a copy of the power of attorney, and suggested doing a drop off ex parte petition to have Mr. Batsuuri step in. Mrs. Murray stated that she cannot afford counsel, that no one is willing to take her case, that she is unable to find counsel and that the reason power of attorney was needed is because years before the Houston's bills had fallen into great problems. Mrs. Murray also stated that as she was trying to pull away, she found out that the basic bills are overdue, that the property taxes were paid late, that the trash bill will probably go into a lien and that Kaiser has not been paid. Mrs. Murray also expressed concern about Camille and Tom's ongoing safety, stated that Tom was found wondering, that she asked the siblings to step in years ago, but they were not willing to in the three years that she was serving the family, that now there are huge claims against her, but there is no proof of those claims and that she will no longer act in any way. In addition, Mrs. Murray stated that Adult Protective Services has been involved since December of 2022 and that they were strongly encouraged to file a conservatorship. The court explained its role with respect to acting on the petition that is before it, and noted that it does not have a conservatorship petition before it and that Mr. Houston is now being taken care of in the least restrictive way. The Petition for Misuse of Power of Attorney, etc., the Petition for Revocation of Authority of Attorney-In-Fact, etc., and status of mediation are continued to March 11, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Verified Petition For 1)Misuse Of Power Of Attorney Under Probate Code Section 4231.5, 4540 And 4541 2)Revocation Of Authority Of Respondent As Attorney In Fact Under Probate Code Section 4541(D) 3) Accounting And Surcharge By Respondent As Attorney In Fact For A Principal Under Probate Code Section 4541(C) 4) Elder Financial Abuse 5) Negligence/Negligence Per Se
Off Calendar Sep-27-2023 Continued To Jan-08-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Ingrid Evans appeared via zoom for Camille Houston (petitioner).
Jerome Garchik appeared in person for Tara Houston (objector).
Jennifer Nicoletto appeared via zoom for Darice Murray-McKee (petitioner).

Discussion was held regarding Mr. Houston's care, that moving him to another care facility that could benefit him and the family. The Court authorizes Mr. Schmere to move Mr. Houston to another care facility.

The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than December 15, 2023. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by December 27, 2023. The matter is continued to January 08, 2024 and the Parties are to return on January 08, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

The joint status report is to address status of mediation, any outstanding discovery issues and timelines, what the claims are, the factual issues, the time estimate of the evidentiary hearing, who the witnesses will be and time estimates of witness testimony.

Judge: Ross Moody: Clerk: A. Datt: Deputy: Maloney: Not reported.
